summary_en: Culture Convenience Club to acquire all Jimoty shares and warrants via tender offer at 1,420 yen/share. Tender offer initiates squeeze-out procedure to make Jimoty a wholly-owned subsidiary, followed by transfer of some shares to Jimoty CEO Takahiro Kato (holding 10.36% as of 31-Dec-2025) at same price. Not classified as MBO as acquirer is third party, not management acting on own behalf.
title_en: Notice regarding commencement of tender offer for shares and stock acquisition rights of Jimoty, Inc. (TSE Growth Code: 7082) by Culture Convenience Club Inc.
translation_en_brief: Culture Convenience Club Inc. announced on 15 May 2026 that it has decided to acquire all ordinary shares and stock acquisition rights of Jimoty, Inc. (TSE Growth: 7082) via public tender offer. The tender price is 1,420 yen per share. Following a successful tender offer, the acquirer intends to execute a squeeze-out procedure to make Jimoty a wholly-owned subsidiary. After the squeeze-out is completed and regulatory approval for suspension of securities report filing is obtained, Culture Convenience Club will transfer a portion of the acquired shares to Takahiro Kato, Jimoty's representative director and CEO (current holding: 992,000 shares plus 136 warrants representing 27,200 shares, or 10.36% of issued shares), at the same tender offer price. The acquirer has agreed with Kato on these terms but details of share quantity and other matters remain undetermined pending the formal tender offer filing. The disclosure explicitly states this does not constitute a management buyout (MBO) because the acquirer is a third party, not management acting independently. The tender offer is scheduled to commence on 18 May 2026 with detailed terms in the formal tender offer registration statement.
